b_findings.text: Cross-channel skew window — wheel and plugin distribution channels update asynchronously; spec frames the change as atomic PR but rollout splits across channels.
R10 grep-only acceptance verifies prose presence, not skill-behavior degradation across the cross-channel skew matrix.
Exit-2 user-action instruction ('ask the user to re-invoke with a disambiguated slug') has no autonomous-mode fallback for overnight runner.

Phase 1 ships resolver returning ambiguous status via cortex-resolve-backlog-item but the footgun-source (update_item._find_item) is only fixed in Phase 2 — Phase 1 done / Phase 2 not started is a coherent failure mode.
R2/R3 Phase 1 acceptance tests new branches against a controlled synthetic; canonical CURATED_INPUTS regression coverage of new branches lands in Phase 2 — test breadth lags by a phase.
